TECH INSTALLATION REFERENCE

Knowledge Base & Troubleshooting Guide

Step-by-step guides with official screenshots, real-world troubleshooting, and expert tips

📋 Device Types 💻 Windows PC Guide 🖥 iGEL Thin Client Guide 🖨 Printer Guide 📋 RFx Q&A ❓ FAQ & Troubleshooting
📋
PC & THIN CLIENT REFRESH PROJECT

RFx Event Q&A — Official Answers

These are the official Walmart answers to vendor questions submitted during the PC and Thin Client Refresh RFx bidding process. Think of these as answers straight from the project managers. Click any question to expand.

🗺 Can you provide a complete site list for all locations included in this RFP? ▾
Yes — site lists have been provided.
🔢 Can you confirm site-level hardware counts for PCs and Thin Clients? ▾
Yes, counts will be broken out by site.
🔒 Are there any specialty or restricted-access sites (e.g., high-security, union-regulated, or remote regions)? ▾
Yes. You will need to coordinate with Pharmacy and Asset Protection to gain access to those areas.
📅 When is the project start date? ▾
Based on hardware readiness. The project follows the holiday freeze periods for Sam's Club and Walmart Stores, then resumes after January 1st.
🗓 What are the expected working days per week? ▾
Since these are 1-for-1 swaps that can be done while the store is open, work can be done Monday through Friday.
🕘 What are the expected working hours or allowable installation windows? ▾
Work can be done during the day, Monday through Friday, up to 9:00 PM.
🚫 Are there defined blackout periods aside from the holiday season? ▾
Follow the standard Walmart blackout dates — Walmart will provide the specific list.
💊 Are there store-specific restrictions for high-traffic or pharmacy locations? ▾
You will need to work with the pharmacy team to schedule and gain access to pharmacy areas. Pharmacy installs normally happen before store opening or after closing.
📦 Will hardware be shipped directly to each site, or to a staging facility first? ▾
Hardware comes from a 3rd-party location (like Berkley or BNSF). The vendor works closely with them to ensure hardware arrives on-site just in time for each install date.
⚙️ Are there any staging or pre-deployment configuration requirements? ▾
All staging is handled by a 3rd party before shipping. Devices arrive pre-imaged. However, the technician will choose the proper 'role' on-site during installation, which pulls down the needed settings and triggers up to two reboots — typically less than 5 minutes total.
🔌 Are there any required materials or accessories (cables, mounts, brackets, peripherals)? ▾
No. Nothing extra is needed beyond what comes with the device. Keyboards and mice are included in the swap. Monitors stay.
📬 Will the vendor receive advance notice of hardware availability and delivery schedules? ▾
Yes. The vendor works with the 3rd-party consolidator (like BNSF or Berkley) on delivery schedules based on install dates.
🚚 Who is responsible for freight charges, pickup scheduling, and bill of lading creation? ▾
The 3rd-party consolidator handles all freight.
♻️ How are deinstalled units handled? ▾
Take them to Claims and follow the normal Walmart used-asset return process. Reuse Walmart pallets and packaging — you don't need to bring your own.
📷 What are the requirements for installation photos? ▾
Follow the normal Walmart standard documentation process.
📊 Is daily reporting into QuickBase performed per site, per device, or per shift? ▾
Yes, QuickBase reporting is required.
🛠 What does Level 1 and Level 2 support mean for this project? ▾
Level 1 = Hardware troubleshooting following the WM-provided steps (loose cables, reboots, login issues). Level 2 = Network issues and cable testing. No MAC address entry is required — expectation is to troubleshoot until the device is functional.
🔄 Is vendor support required post-installation, and for how long? ▾
Follow the standard Walmart procedures for any project.
🧪 Will there be a pilot or proof-of-concept phase before full rollout? ▾
Yes, there will be a pilot phase. Install time estimates will be confirmed at that stage.
📈 Do you have an expected run rate (installs per day per tech)? ▾
The rate should be enough to complete by October 15th, with 2 weeks for cleanup, then a pause until after January 1st. Exact rate determined at pilot.
🏁 Are there any completion milestones besides the October 15, 2026 deadline? ▾
No intermediate milestones — the main deadline is October 15, 2026.
🤝 Are there other vendors performing related work we need to coordinate with? ▾
Only the 3rd-party consolidator/shipper.
💊 Do vendor technicians need HIPAA certifications or a Business Associate Agreement (BAA) for pharmacy areas? ▾
Not at this time. No patient information is being saved or accessed during the install.
❌ How are Out of Box Failures (OBFs) managed? ▾
Notify the 3rd-party consolidator/shipper and request a reship of the replacement unit.
✅ Can multiple units be worked on at the same time at a site? ▾
Yes — as long as at least one device is left operational for the store at all times.
🏪 Are Walmart and Sam's Club sites combined in scope, or tracked separately? ▾
They are tracked and scheduled separately. Sam's Club was bid separately.
⏳ What is the estimated install time for a new PC and a new thin client? ▾
To be determined at the pilot stage.
🔁 What does 'revisit trip + device' mean in the pricing context? ▾
It means: if a replacement unit needs to be shipped and a technician must return to the site to install it, both the return trip cost and the device cost are counted separately.
👷 On Step 3 of the Windows Workstation guide, it says allow 15 minutes for provisioning — do techs need to stay hands-on? ▾
Yes, technicians should remain on-site during the provisioning window.
🗃 Will equipment arrive in bulk or in phases? ▾
The vendor works with the 3rd-party consolidator/shipper (like Berkley or BNSF) for just-in-time delivery to each site.
📞 Who coordinates pharmacist availability for pharmacy installs — vendor or the store? ▾
Both — the vendor and Walmart share communication to sites.
🗓 Will installs be sequenced by Walmart, or does the vendor control the schedule? ▾
The vendor controls the schedule within the October 15th deadline.
📋 Will Walmart provide blackout dates vendors need to work around? ▾
Yes, Walmart will provide the blackout dates.
❓ What is the expected project start date? ▾
Based on hardware readiness — TBD.
🏷
REFERENCE

What Kind of Device Are You Installing?

Check the label or hostname to identify which type of device you have before starting.

Name/Hostname Purpose Where It Goes Type Guide
CBLTraining workstationBreak room, training areaWindows PC↓ PC Guide
MPTMulti-purpose workstationElectronics, Service Desk, Sporting GoodsWindows PC↓ PC Guide
MPCManager workstationManager officesWindows PC↓ PC Guide
RXCO / RXPCPharmacy register & workstationPharmacy departmentWindows PC↓ PC Guide
HP t420 / t430Thin client — streams from serverCheckouts, General Access, Vision CenteriGEL Thin Client↓ iGEL Guide
Dell Wyse 3040Thin client — streams from serverCheckouts, General AccessiGEL Thin Client↓ iGEL Guide
💻
GUIDE 1 — CBL · MPT · MPC · RXCO · RXPC

Windows Workstation Installation

For all standard Windows desktop computers — the full-size units that sit on desks and connect to monitors, keyboards, and mice.

S1
🔌 Power Off and Fully Unplug the Old Workstation ▾
Hold the power button on the old computer until all lights go out and the screen goes completely dark.

Then disconnect everything from the back — in this order helps prevent mistakes:
1. Network cable (unclick the tab and pull)
2. Monitor cable
3. Keyboard and mouse
4. Any other peripherals (scanners, signature pads, etc.)
5. Power cable (always last)

Move the old unit aside carefully.
💡 TIP  Take a photo of the back of the old computer before unplugging — it shows you exactly where each cable goes on the new unit.
S2
📦 Place New Workstation and Connect Everything ▾
Set the new unit in the same location as the old one. Connect in this order:

1. Monitor cable (push firmly until it seats — some require screws tightened)
2. Keyboard
3. Mouse
4. Any peripherals (scanner, signature pad, printer USB)
5. Network cable — push until you hear or feel a click
6. Power cable — last!
💡 TIP  Connect ALL peripherals before pressing the power button. Hardware is detected during startup — if you plug in the keyboard after boot, it may not register until the next reboot.
S3
⏳ Power On — Then Wait Patiently ▾
Press the power button once and step back.

• CBL, MPT, MPC: Allow approximately 15 minutes for Windows provisioning to complete
• RXCO, RXPC (pharmacy): Allow up to 45–60 minutes — pharmacy applications are large and take additional time

The system is pulling down configurations, group policies, and application packages from Walmart's servers. It may look like nothing is happening — but it is. Leave it alone.
💡 TIP  If the hard drive activity light on the front of the PC is flickering, it's working. A computer that's completely frozen with no disk activity for more than 30 minutes is a different story.
⚠️ WATCH OUT  Do NOT power off the computer during provisioning. Interrupting this process can corrupt the machine image and require a full helpdesk reimaging.
S4
🔑 Test Login — Password MUST Be ALL CAPS ▾
Once the Windows desktop appears, have the store manager or an associate test login:

Format: firstname.s0XXXX
Example: jdoe.s01234 (note the zero before store number)

The password must be typed in ALL CAPITAL LETTERS. This is a hard requirement of Walmart's authentication system. If Caps Lock is off, hold Shift while typing every character.

If login fails on first try, check caps, check network cable, and try again with a different associate account to rule out an account-specific issue.
💡 TIP  The #1 reason new workstation logins fail at install time: the technician types the password in lowercase. Verify Caps Lock is ON before every login attempt.
S5
📸 Document — Serial Number, Photo, Tracking Sheet ▾
Before leaving the site, complete all documentation:

1. Photograph the running workstation (screen visible, unit in position)
2. Record the serial number from the sticker on the side or bottom of the unit
3. Update the tracking sheet with the new serial and mark installation complete

Do not leave without the serial number. There is no recovery if you leave the site without it except calling the store and hoping someone can find it.
💡 TIP  The serial number sticker is usually on the side panel, the bottom, or sometimes on a pull-out tag. It starts with a letter (like 8CG, 4GM, etc.) and is 10-12 characters long.
🖥
GUIDE 2 — HP t420 · HP t430 · DELL WYSE 3040 — WITH OFFICIAL SCREENSHOTS

iGEL Thin Client Setup

Thin clients are small, book-sized devices that don't store programs locally — they stream everything from Walmart's servers. Steps 1–7 apply to brand-new "Ignition" devices only.

⛔
Do NOT connect to Walmart Wi-Fi during setup!
Initial setup requires a physical ethernet cable. Connecting wirelessly will break enrollment and may require a full device reset.
📋 Steps 1–7 apply only to Ignition (brand-new) devices
If the device has been configured before, skip to Step 8.
S1
🔌 Plug In via Ethernet — NO Wi-Fi! ▾
The thin client must be hardwired to a network switch via an RJ-45 ethernet cable before anything else. This is required even if the device is approved for wireless operation after setup — initial enrollment only works over a wired connection.

Connect the ethernet cable to the port on the back of the device and to a wall jack or switch. Then connect the power supply and power on the device.

⛔ Do NOT connect to Walmart Wi-Fi under any circumstances during initial setup. Connecting wirelessly during enrollment will break the process and may require a full device reset.
💡 TIP  Think of it like checking in at a hotel desk — you get your key card (enrollment) from the front desk (wired network) before you're allowed to use the Wi-Fi.
S2
⌨️ Power On → Rapidly Tap ESC to Enter BIOS ▾
As soon as the device powers on and the screen lights up, immediately start tapping the ESC key rapidly — over and over without stopping. You have only a few seconds before the OS boots past the BIOS window.

You are trying to reach the BIOS startup menu. Once you see the menu appear, either press F10 or use the arrow keys to highlight "Computer Setup" and press Enter.

If you miss the window and the iGEL desktop loads instead, power the device off and try again — tap faster this time.
💡 TIP  Tap ESC like you're trying to skip an unskippable video ad — fast, repeated tapping is the only way in.
⚠️ WATCH OUT  If the device boots fully into iGEL before you get the menu, that's fine — just power it off and on and try again. You haven't broken anything.
S3
⚙️ Navigate to Advanced → Power-On Options ▾
Inside Computer Setup (BIOS), use your keyboard arrow keys to navigate to the Advanced menu at the top of the screen. Your mouse will not work inside the BIOS.

Arrow down and select "Power-On Options" and press Enter to open it.
💡 TIP  BIOS menus are keyboard-only. Left/right arrow keys move between top menu sections; up/down move within a section. Enter selects.
S4
🔍 Confirm 'After Power Loss' Setting ▾
Inside Power-On Options, look for the setting called "After Power Loss".

This setting controls what the thin client does when the store has a power outage. We need it set to Previous State so devices automatically turn back on when power is restored — critical for store operations.

• If it already shows Previous State → skip to Step 6
• If it shows Off → continue to Step 5
💡 TIP  Why this matters: If a store loses power and this is set to 'Off', every single thin client needs to be manually turned on one by one after power returns. With 'Previous State', they all come back automatically.
S5
🔄 Change 'After Power Loss' to Previous State ▾
Use the ← or → arrow keys to change the After Power Loss setting from Off to Previous State.

Then press F10 to accept the change before moving on.
💡 TIP  Use left/right arrow keys — not up/down — to cycle through the option values for this setting.
⚠️ WATCH OUT  Press F10 to accept the change before navigating away, otherwise the change will be silently discarded.
S6
💾 Save Changes and Exit BIOS ▾
Navigate (using arrow keys) to the File menu at the top of the Computer Setup screen.

Arrow down and select "Save Changes and Exit" then press Enter.
💡 TIP  Always save before exiting. Closing the BIOS without saving means all your changes are lost and you start over.
S7
✅ Confirm Save — Device Reboots ▾
A confirmation dialog will appear. Select Yes and press Enter.

The device will now reboot automatically. Steps 1–7 are now complete for Ignition (brand-new) devices. The device will boot up, contact Walmart's network, and move into the iGEL enrollment phase automatically.
💡 TIP  Once you click Yes, sit back. The device handles everything automatically from here through the next few steps.
S8
🌐 Wait for iGEL Desktop and Device Setup Window ▾
After rebooting, the device will connect to Walmart's UMS (Universal Management Suite) server and may reboot one more time automatically. This is normal.

After the reboot, you should see the iGEL desktop environment load, and the Device Setup menu should open automatically.

If the Device Setup menu does not appear automatically, look for a "Device Setup" icon on the desktop and double-click it.
💡 TIP  UMS = Universal Management Suite = Walmart's central server that configures and manages all thin clients. When the device contacts UMS, it downloads its configuration automatically.
⚠️ WATCH OUT  If the Device Setup icon is missing AND no menu appeared after 5+ minutes, check that the ethernet cable is still firmly plugged in and reboot the device once.
S9
🏢 Confirm Domain and Site Number (Auto-Filled) ▾
The Device Setup window will show two fields at the top — Domain and Site Number — already filled in based on the network the device is connected to. You don't need to type anything here.

Important note: If you are in the WMSC domain, the setup menu will display your domain as US. This is correct — do not try to change it.
💡 TIP  These fields are pulled automatically from the network. Verify they look right but do not manually edit them unless instructed by the helpdesk.
S10
🏪 Select Site Type from Dropdown ▾
Click the Site Type dropdown and select the type that matches the location you are working in:

Select ThisWhen Installing At...
Distribution CenterA warehouse or DC location
Stores (Walmart)Any Walmart store
HOMEOFFICECorporate or home office location
Clubs (Sams)Any Sam's Club location
💡 TIP  Cross-check the Site Type against the store address in your work order if you're unsure. Walmart uses 'Stores', Sam's Club uses 'Clubs'.
S11
🎭 Select Device Role and Click Continue ▾
Click the Device Role dropdown and choose the role for this specific device:

• General Access — standard role used in most store departments
• Vision Center — used specifically for the optical/vision department

After selecting the correct role, click Continue.

Note: Some roles require an IGEL OS upgrade. If so, you'll see a prompt — covered in Step 13.
💡 TIP  If you're unsure which role, check the device's intended location. A device going to the optical counter = Vision Center. Everything else = General Access.
⚠️ WATCH OUT  Choosing the wrong role will configure the device for the wrong use case. If you realize you made a mistake, click Cancel on the confirmation screen to go back.
S12
📋 Read Confirmation Dialog and Click Yes ▾
A confirmation dialog will appear showing the new hostname that will be assigned to this device during setup. This is the name the device will have in Walmart's systems going forward.

Read it carefully to confirm it matches the device and location you're setting up. If everything looks correct, click Yes. If something looks wrong, click Cancel to go back and correct your selections.
💡 TIP  This is your final checkpoint before the device commits to its configuration. Take 10 seconds to read the hostname — it saves hours of re-enrollment work later.
S13
⬆️ (If Prompted) OS Upgrade — Do Not Touch the Device ▾
If the selected role requires an IGEL OS upgrade, you will see an upgrade notification dialog. Do not touch the device.

The upgrade runs entirely in the background. A notification will appear when it is complete and ready to reboot. This process can take 10–30 minutes.

If you do not see an upgrade prompt at all, your device is already on the correct OS version — proceed to Step 14.
💡 TIP  The device is essentially downloading and installing a new operating system brain. This is normal and expected for certain roles.
⚠️ WATCH OUT  NEVER power off the device during an OS upgrade. If the screen looks frozen, check for disk activity and wait at least 30 minutes before taking any action. Interrupting an upgrade can brick the device.
S14
🎉 Click OK — Final Reboot ▾
You will receive a "Confirmation finished" message. Click OK to continue.

This is your last chance to re-run the setup menu if you need to make any changes. Otherwise, reboot the device to complete the setup.

Important: The device may not display the correct role screen on its very first boot after setup. More than one reboot is sometimes required before the device fully applies its profile from UMS. If it doesn't look right after the first reboot, reboot again and wait a few minutes.
💡 TIP  Think of it like a new employee's first day — sometimes they need a couple of shifts before they're fully up to speed. A second reboot after setup is completely normal.
S15
🖥 Device Is Ready for Use ▾
After the final reboot, the thin client should now display the correct login interface for its assigned role. The device is fully enrolled, configured, and ready for associates to use.

The screen will look different depending on the assigned role — General Access shows the standard Walmart login interface, while Vision Center shows an optical-department-specific interface.

Before leaving the site: photograph the device, record the serial number from the label on the unit, and update your work order documentation.
💡 TIP  Serial number is always on a white sticker on the device itself — usually on the bottom or back. It's your proof of installation.
❓
REAL-WORLD TECHNICIAN TROUBLESHOOTING

FAQ & Common Problems

Compiled from field technician reports, IGEL community forums, Dell support documentation, and real-world store deployment experience. Click any question to expand.

POWER & BOOT
🔌 Thin client turns on for a second then immediately powers off ▾
This is a documented issue with the Dell Wyse 3040 — caused by the BIOS "AC Recovery" setting being in mini-boot mode.

Fix: Enter BIOS setup (tap F2 at boot), go to Power Management, and set AC Recovery to Power On.

This is the exact same "After Power Loss" setting from Steps 1–7 of the iGEL guide. If you already configured this during setup and it's still happening, try swapping the power adapter with a known-good one from another device of the same model.
🟡 HP t430 powers on but shows solid amber/orange LED, won't boot ▾
A solid amber LED on an HP thin client indicates a power or hardware fault.

Troubleshooting steps in order:
1. Unplug the power adapter completely, wait 30 seconds, plug back in
2. Try a different power adapter from another t430 (power adapter swap test)
3. Reseat any accessible memory modules
4. If none of those work, the unit has an internal fault — document the serial number and call the helpdesk for a replacement swap

Do not attempt to open the thin client case or swap internal parts in the field.
⚫ Screen stays completely black after powering on (thin client) ▾
Black screen on power-on is almost always one of these three things:

1. Monitor cable not seated: Power off the thin client, unplug the display cable from both ends, plug it back in firmly, power back on.

2. Wrong display port: HP t430 requires DisplayPort for dual monitors — two HDMI ports will NOT work for dual display. Try moving the cable to the DisplayPort if using HDMI.

3. Monitor needs to be on first: For Dell Wyse 3040, the monitor must already be powered on when the thin client boots. Turn the monitor on, then boot the thin client.

Last resort: Try a completely different monitor to rule out a dead display.
💻 Windows workstation shows black screen after boot, then goes to BIOS ▾
This usually means the BIOS boot order changed, or the hard drive is not being detected. This can happen if the unit was dropped or mishandled during transit.

Enter BIOS (F2 at startup on Dell), go to Boot Sequence, and ensure the internal hard drive is listed first. If the hard drive doesn't appear in the list at all, the drive may be unseated or failed — call the helpdesk.
NETWORK & VLAN
🌐 Device powers on normally but can't contact UMS / won't enroll ▾
UMS enrollment failure is almost always a network problem. Work through these in order:

1. Physical check first: Unplug and re-plug the ethernet cable. The port should have an LED indicator — if it's not lit, the cable or jack has a problem.

2. Try a different wall jack: Not all wall jacks in a store are active. Try the jack that the previous device was using — you know that one works.

3. VLAN mismatch: This is the most common non-obvious cause. If the network port isn't on the correct VLAN for thin clients, the device can't reach Walmart's UMS servers even though it appears connected. Call the helpdesk with the wall jack ID/location and ask them to verify the VLAN assignment on that port.

4. Wrong cable: Grab a cable from a working device and try it. Bad cables are surprisingly common.
🔗 Device is connected to network but can't reach Citrix / virtual desktop (enrolled fine) ▾
This is different from the enrollment/UMS problem. If the device enrolled correctly (hostname changed, Device Setup completed) but the virtual desktop session fails to load:

The VLAN for Vision Center devices is different from General Access. If the device was enrolled as Vision Center but is on a General Access network port, it won't be able to reach the correct resources.

Contact the helpdesk with the device hostname, site number, and role. Ask them to verify the VLAN assignment for the specific wall jack matches the device role.
🖥 Windows workstation gets IP address but can't authenticate to domain ▾
If the workstation can ping the network but login fails with a domain error, this is almost always a VLAN or DNS issue rather than a password problem.

Things to check:
1. Confirm the network cable is in the same jack the old machine was in
2. If the machine has an IP address starting with 169.254.x.x, it has no valid network connection (APIPA address) — check the cable and port
3. Call the helpdesk and ask them to verify DNS resolution and VLAN assignment for that port
LOGIN ISSUES
🔑 Login fails — 'incorrect username or password' ▾
This is the single most common installation problem. Check in this exact order:

1. Caps Lock: Walmart domain passwords must be ALL CAPITALS. Press Caps Lock and look for the indicator light on the keyboard. If there's no light, try pressing it and attempt login again.

2. Username format: Must be exactly firstname.s0XXXX — lowercase first name, then a period, then the letter 's', then a zero, then the four-digit store number. Example: jdoe.s01234. A missing zero or wrong store number is a common mistake.

3. Network cable: If the cable is unplugged or in the wrong jack, the computer can't reach the domain controller to verify credentials. Check that the port LED is lit.

4. Try a different account: Have another associate try their credentials. If a second account also fails, it's a machine or network issue. If the second account works, the first account has a specific problem (locked, expired, etc.).
⚠️ Login error: 'The trust relationship between this workstation and the primary domain failed' ▾
This specific error means the computer's secure connection to Walmart's Active Directory domain has been broken — usually because the machine sat offline too long or was reimaged incorrectly.

This requires helpdesk intervention. You cannot fix this yourself in the field without domain admin credentials. Call the helpdesk and give them the computer hostname and site number. They can remotely repair the secure channel or re-join the machine to the domain.

Technical context: Every domain-joined computer has a machine account password that auto-rotates every 30 days. If the machine was offline when the rotation happened, the stored password on the PC doesn't match what's in Active Directory.
🏥 Pharmacy workstation (RXCO/RXPC) boots fine but pharmacy apps aren't loading ▾
Pharmacy applications are the largest and most complex deployed to any store workstation. They can take 30–60 minutes after first login to fully download and configure.

Steps:
1. Verify the ethernet cable is connected and the port LED is lit — pharmacy apps download from the network
2. Log in with a pharmacy account (pharmacy apps require pharmacy credentials, not general store credentials)
3. Wait a full 30 minutes before calling the helpdesk — if you call before the apps finish installing, you'll be told to wait
4. If nothing has changed after 45 minutes, call the helpdesk
iGEL SPECIFIC
🔄 Device enrolled but boots to wrong role / blank screen with cursor ▾
This is the single most common post-enrollment issue reported by field technicians. It is normal and expected on first boot.

The device is still downloading its full profile from UMS. The profile download can lag behind the boot sequence by 1-3 reboots.

Fix: Reboot the device. If it still looks wrong after the second reboot, wait 5 minutes on the desktop and reboot a third time. If it still hasn't resolved after 3 reboots and 15 minutes, call the helpdesk with the hostname and site number.
🖥 iGEL Device Setup menu shows wrong site number ▾
If the Device Setup menu shows an incorrect site number, the device contacted the network on the wrong VLAN or connected to the wrong network segment.

Do not proceed past this point — enrolling under the wrong site number means the device gets configured for a completely different store. Click Cancel, verify the ethernet cable is in the correct jack for this store, and reopen the Device Setup menu. If the wrong site number persists, call the helpdesk before continuing.
⬆️ OS upgrade appears stuck / no progress after 30+ minutes ▾
IGEL OS upgrades are background processes that provide very little visual feedback. The screen may look completely frozen while the upgrade is actually running.

What to watch: Any storage or network activity indicator on the device (LEDs, status in the IGEL setup window). As long as some activity is occurring, the upgrade is working.

Wait 45 minutes before taking any action. If absolutely no change has occurred after 45 minutes — no status change, no indicators, no network activity — call the helpdesk before rebooting. Rebooting mid-upgrade can require a full device reflash.
🌐 'Cannot connect to remote management server' error on thin client ▾
This error means the thin client cannot reach the UMS server. It's a network/firewall issue.

UMS communication uses port 30001. If the network port or firewall is blocking this port, enrollment fails with this exact message.

Field steps: Try a different wall jack (preferably one you've seen work). Ensure the ethernet cable is fully clicked in. Call the helpdesk to verify port 30001 is not being blocked on the port you're connected to.
HARDWARE
🖱 Keyboard or mouse not responding after connecting ▾
Almost always a USB connection issue:

1. Unplug the device and plug it into a different USB port (try both front and back ports)
2. Make sure the USB plug is fully inserted — they can feel inserted but not make full contact
3. Test with a known-good keyboard/mouse from another workstation
4. If the replacement also doesn't work, the USB ports themselves may have a problem — reboot the workstation

Note: Some older Dell OptiPlex models will not recognize a USB device that is plugged in after boot until the next reboot.
🖨 Store printer not appearing on workstation after install ▾
Store printers are deployed automatically through Walmart's print management system upon successful domain login. If a printer is missing:

1. Wait: Printer assignment can take 10-15 minutes after login while Group Policies apply
2. Check other workstations: If other PCs in the same store can see the printer, the issue is with the new workstation's network connectivity or profile download
3. If all workstations lost the printer: The problem is with the printer itself or the print server — not the new workstation. The workstation install is actually complete
4. Log out and back in: Sometimes printer assignments apply on the second login
🌡 Thin client feels very hot — is this normal? ▾
HP t430 and Dell Wyse 3040 thin clients run significantly warmer than you might expect — this is by design. They use passive or semi-passive cooling to stay quiet.

Normal: The device feeling warm or even hot to the touch after running for hours
Not normal: The device smelling burnt, showing smoke, or being so hot you can't hold your hand on it for 3+ seconds

Always ensure: At least 2 inches of clearance on all sides, not mounted inside a fully-enclosed cabinet without ventilation, and not stacked directly on top of another device.
DOCS & PROCESS
📸 What documentation is required at each site? ▾
Required at every installation:

• Photo of the new device running (screen on, device visible in its installed position)
• Serial number recorded (from sticker on device — usually starts with 8CG, 4GM, CNU, etc.)
• Successful login confirmed by a store manager or associate
• Work order updated and marked complete

For thin clients: Also verify the hostname shown in the device's setup or system info matches the expected name from your work order.
🆘 When should I stop trying to fix it and call the helpdesk? ▾
Call the helpdesk immediately for:
• Any BIOS changes you're unsure about
• Wrong site number during enrollment
• "Trust relationship" domain error
• Device enrolled at wrong site
• Pharmacy workstation not loading apps after 45+ minutes
• Request to re-enroll a device
• Any VLAN change requests

Try yourself first (then call if still stuck after 15 min):
• Black screens and loose cables
• Wrong passwords / caps lock issues
• Waiting out OS upgrades
• Multiple reboots after setup
• Peripheral reconnection

Rule of thumb: If it involves changing anything in the network configuration or Active Directory, that's always a helpdesk call.
🔁 I installed the device at the wrong site — what do I do? ▾
Stop work immediately and call the helpdesk. Do not attempt to re-enroll the device yourself.

Enrolling a device under the wrong site causes it to receive the wrong hostname, wrong VLAN assignments, and wrong application profiles. It can also create conflicts in Walmart's device management system that affect the intended store.

The helpdesk team can remotely wipe and re-enroll the device under the correct site. Give them the device's current hostname (shown in the IGEL setup or iGEL system info), the incorrect site number it enrolled under, and the correct site number it should be at.
🖨
LEXMARK / XEROX

Printer Installation Guide

This guide covers how to install, configure, and troubleshoot Lexmark and Xerox laser printers at Walmart and Sam's Club locations. Explained step-by-step in plain language.

🖨 Printer Model Quick Reference

Before you start, check the box or the label on the front of the printer to identify which model you have:

Part #ModelDescription
25B3361MX722adeLarge multifunction, touch screen
50G0620MS826deHigh-speed black & white laser
38S0109MS632dweCompact B&W laser, no touch screen
38S0105MX632adweMultifunction (print/copy/scan), no touch screen
47C0528CX735adseColor multifunction, large touch screen
50M7080CX635adweColor multifunction, smaller touch screen
50M0020CS531dwCompact color laser, no touch screen
💡 Quick tip: Models starting with M = black & white only. Models starting with C = color. Models with X in the name = multifunction (print + copy + scan).
📸 Printer Model Photos
MS826de
B&W • Touch screen
CS531dw
COLOR • Compact
CX635adwe
COLOR • MFP scan lid
MX632adwe
B&W • MFP scan lid
MX722ade
B&W • Large MFP
CX735adse
COLOR • Large MFP
💡 Quick tip: Models starting with M = black & white only. Models starting with C = color. Models with X in the name = multifunction (print + copy + scan). Any model with a lid on top can scan.
1
Getting Access to the Printer Tool (Rollouts)

Think of "Rollouts" like a special backstage door into the store's network system — you need a key card to get in. Here's how to request yours:

  1. Open a browser and go to https://walmartglobal.service-now.com/wm_sp/
  2. Select Active Directory
  3. In the dropdown, select "Modify Active Directory Group Membership" → click Next
  4. In the Group name field, type exactly: StoreSys-AppTeam-ProcID-rollout
  5. Enter your User ID, select "Add users"
  6. Business Justification: "Access is required to update MAC address information for new hardware in stores/clubs."
  7. Click Next to submit, then notify your manager to approve it promptly
📸 Screenshots — ServiceNow access request steps:
Step 1: Select "Active Directory"
Step 2: Select "Modify Active Directory Group Membership"
Step 3: Enable the toggle and expand options
Step 4: Enter the group name exactly as shown
Step 5: Enter your user ID in the members box
Step 6: Select "Add users" from the dropdown
Step 7: Paste the exact business justification text shown below
⚠️ Note: You cannot install printers without this access. Request it early — approval takes time.
2
Logging In to the Printer Administration Tool

You'll use PuTTY — a program that lets you type commands to the store's server. Think of it like a remote control for the server in the back room.

METHOD A — Via Rollouts Menu (most common)
  1. Open PuTTY, enter one of these hostnames: Oser2009922 / Oser2009923 / Oser2009924 / Oser2009925
  2. Click Open, log in with your credentials
  3. Type: jumpto -u rollouts -s isp.s0XXXX.us (replace XXXX with the 4-digit store number)
  4. Press Enter, then type 5 and press Enter
  5. Navigate: Thin Client → Install Network Devices → Printer Installation Menu
METHOD B — Direct Command (sudo/root access)

With sudo: sudo printer.menu  |  With root: printer.menu

3
Understanding the Printer Administration Menu

Once logged in you'll see the main menu. Think of it like the control panel for every printer in the store. Here's what each option means:

OptionWhat it Does
SYSTEM / BUSINESS / LOCATIONInfo only — verify LOCATION matches the store you're actually at
SPOOLERUp = printing works. Down = no printing until fixed
InstallAdd a brand-new printer not yet in the system
ManageModify, test, update firmware, or reinstall an existing printer
Printer StatusView online/offline status, firmware versions, print queues
Cycle SpoolerRestart the print service — use if printing is broken but everything else looks fine
✅ Good to know: Every time you open printer.menu it automatically checks file permissions, script versions, and service health. It's like a car running its own diagnostic every time you start it.
📟 What the Printer Administration Menu looks like on screen:
⬆️ This is the actual terminal screen you see after logging in. Always verify LOCATION matches the store you're physically at. If SPOOLER shows "Down", run Cycle Spooler before installing.
📟 Manage Menu — Printer List & Options:
Printer list (varies by store):
Single-printer options:
After selecting "Modify" — sub-options:
⬆️ The actual printer list varies by store. These are real screenshots from a Walmart store install.
4
Installing a New Printer — The Two Key Questions

Before installing, you need to answer two questions. The menu will ask you both — you just need to know the right answers.

Q1: Does it have a Queue?

A "queue" is a reserved spot in line on the server. Main office/department printers like the pharmacy laser, hub printer, or invoice printer have queues. Specialty devices like bakery or deli printers do not.

Q2: Static (VLAN20) or Dynamic (VLAN25)?

Static/VLAN20: The printer always gets the same IP (like a permanent address). Dynamic/VLAN25: IP changes on reboot (like a hotel room reassigned each visit). Newer Walmart installs use VLAN25.

Common Queue Names — Static VLAN20
gl — Primary Pharmacy Laser
gl2–gl10 — Additional Pharmacy
laser_4 — Secondary Laser
laser_hub — Store Hub Printer
LBL — Label Printer
lp_INVOICE — Invoice/Gen Merch
jewelry — Jewelry
rp2 — Pharmacy Report
lp_DO — District Manager
samscc — Sam's Credit Center
Common Queue Names — Dynamic VLAN25
fuel_rec — Fuel Receiving
liquor_rec — Liquor Receiving
pufpr01/pufpr02 — Pick-Up Facility
5
First-Time Startup — Dismissing the New Printer Setup Wizard

Brand-new Lexmark printers show a setup wizard on first power-on. Think of it like the "Welcome" screens on a new phone — you want to skip them, not set anything up:

A
Swipe Left on the welcome screen
B
Choose "Set up Later" — do NOT connect to Wi-Fi or cloud services
C
Select "None" for network type, then Swipe Left again
D
Swipe Left once more, then select "Confirm and Launch"
📸 Real screenshots from a new Lexmark printer startup:
A: Welcome screen — Swipe Left
B: Choose "Set up Later" (not "Set up Now")
C: Anonymous Data — check "None", swipe left
D: Wireless — swipe left past this screen
E: Final screen — click "Confirm and Launch" ✅
⚠️ Warning: Do NOT set up the printer on Wi-Fi. It needs to be wired to the store network and registered through printer.menu — not configured as a standalone device.
6
Finding the IP Address and MAC Address

The IP address is the printer's phone number on the network. The MAC address is its permanent ID. You need both to complete the install. Print them directly from the printer:

📟 No Touch Screen (Ms82x, Cs52x, Cx52x, MS632)

Network Report (IP + MAC):

  1. Down arrow → Settings → OK
  2. Down arrow → Reports → OK
  3. Down arrow → Network → OK
  4. Network Setup Page → OK → Prints!
📱 Touch Screen (Mx52x, Cx62x, Mx72x, MX722, CX635)

Network Report (IP + MAC):

  1. Tap Settings on the front panel
  2. Left menu → scroll to Reports
  3. Right side → tap Network
  4. Tap Network Setup Page → Prints!
📄 Real photos — navigating to & reading the printout:
Button-panel printers (MS826, MS632, MX632) — use the physical arrow and OK buttons:
1. The OK + arrow button panel
2. Home screen — arrow down to Settings
3. Highlight Settings → press OK
4. Scroll to Reports → OK
5. Select Network → OK
6. Network Setup Page → OK → prints!
Also for Asset Report (button-panel models): Settings → Reports → Device → Asset Report
Reports → Device highlighted
Device → Asset Report highlighted
Touch-screen printers (MX722, CX735, CX635) — tap the large front panel:
1. Home screen — tap Settings icon
2. Settings → scroll left to Reports, tap Network
3. Tap "Network Setup Page" → prints!
Real Network Setup Page printout (MX722)
Real Asset Report printout — IP Address and MAC Address are highlighted in yellow:
⬆️ The yellow highlights show exactly where to find the IP Address and MAC Address on the printed report. Write these down before starting the printer.menu install.
7
VLAN25 Troubleshooting — Printer Won't Come Online

If the printer doesn't show "UP" status in PrinterStats, work through these steps. Think of VLAN25 like a specific highway lane — the printer has to be in the right lane to communicate:

1. The install script tells you the first 3 octets a valid VLAN25 IP should start with. If the printer's IP doesn't match those 3 numbers, it's on the wrong network.
2. After checking PrinterStats, if the printer isn't "UP", press Enter and check again — it sometimes needs a moment to respond.
3. Verify the IP shown in the menu exactly matches what was printed on the Network Setup Page.
4. IP is correct but still not UP? Power the printer off, wait 30 seconds, back on. Wait for the display to say "Ready", then check PrinterStats again.
5. IP is wrong? Select the printer in the menu and reinstall it using the correct IP address.
6. Check for a blinking green link light on the back of the printer's network port. No link light = cable or switch port problem.
⚠️ Never cancel the install process mid-run — it can leave the printer in a broken state that's harder to fix.
8
Setting Up Fax and Email (SMTP) on Multifunction Printers

Multifunction models (those with MX or CX in the name that have a scan lid) also need fax and email settings. If your printer can't scan or fax, skip this step.

📠 Fax Setup
  1. Select Fax from the left menu
  2. Select Fax Setup
  3. Enter FAX Name (Department + Store #)
  4. Enter any phone number (even fake if no fax line)
  5. Uncheck all 3 boxes to stop analog alarm
  6. Fax transport = Analog (keep as-is)
  7. Confirm it's set to Fax Server (not just Fax)
  8. Open Fax Server Setup and fill required fields
📧 SMTP Setup (scan-to-email)

Enter these exact values — do not change them:

Primary SMTP Gateway:
smtp-gw1.homeoffice.wal-mart.com
Reply Address:
DoNotReply@wal-mart.com
Subject field can be customized. Only fill blank fields — don't overwrite values that are already correct.
📸 Real screenshots — Fax and SMTP setup in the Lexmark Embedded Web Server:
Fax main page — confirm Fax Mode = "Fax Server"
Fax Server dropdown — select "Fax Server" (not Fax)
Fax Setup — uncheck all 3 boxes highlighted
Fax Server Setup — fill in To Format and Reply Address
E-mail (SMTP) Setup — Primary SMTP Gateway and Reply Address highlighted in yellow
⬆️ The yellow highlights in the SMTP screenshot show exactly which fields to fill in. Don't change anything else on that page.
9
Out of Box Failures (OBF) — When a Brand-New Printer Doesn't Work

Sometimes a new printer arrives broken. This is called an OBF. The most important rule: only a Lexmark engineer can officially declare an OBF — not you or your manager.

📞 Lexmark OBF Contacts — Call in This Order
Eric Barbee
479-903-0621
Eric.Barbee@Lexmark.com
James Ott
479-715-3586
James.Ott@Lexmark.com
Rick Musa
479-685-0157
Rick.Musa@Lexmark.com
A. Call a Lexmark engineer — they troubleshoot and make the official OBF call.
B. If confirmed: reinstall the old printer so the store stays operational while waiting for the replacement.
C. Re-box the failed unit in its original packaging, label it "OBF". Lexmark will overnight a replacement and provide an RMA label.
D. NEVER put an OBF on a Walmart Claims truck. It can get lost and the store gets billed for a printer they never had. Use the Lexmark RMA label to ship it back.
PRINTER FAQ
🔴The printer shows the right IP but still shows as "DOWN" in PrinterStats▾
This is the most common issue on VLAN25 installs. Try this sequence:

1. Power the printer fully off
2. Wait a full 30 seconds
3. Power it back on and wait until the display says "Ready"
4. Check PrinterStats again

If still DOWN after 2 reboots: check for a green link light on the back network port. No link light = cable problem. Link light but still DOWN = the IP may have changed — reprint the Network Setup Page and compare.
🌐The install tool says the IP is not on VLAN25 — what does that mean?▾
The printer is on the wrong network. The tool will show what the first 3 numbers of a valid VLAN25 address should be (e.g., 10.52.15.xxx).

Until the printer's IP starts with those same 3 numbers, it won't work. Fix:
1. Move the network cable to a VLAN25-enabled switch port
2. Power-cycle the printer so it picks up a new IP
3. Reprint the Network Setup Page and verify the new IP matches the VLAN25 range
🖨The print spooler shows "Down" — no printers are working in the store▾
A down spooler means the print service crashed — nothing will print until it's restarted.

From the main menu, select Cycle Spooler. Wait 60 seconds, then check status again.

If it keeps crashing repeatedly during your visit, that's a server issue — escalate to the helpdesk instead of keep cycling it.
🆘Brand-new printer seems dead — won't power on or has obvious physical damage▾
Don't troubleshoot beyond checking the power cable and outlet. The OBF process exists for exactly this.

Call Lexmark: Eric Barbee (479-903-0621), James Ott (479-715-3586), or Rick Musa (479-685-0157).

While waiting: reinstall the old printer so the store can continue working. Never leave a department without a working printer.
📠The fax is showing an alarm or error about the analog line▾
Normal and expected. The fix is simple:

1. Go to Fax → Fax Setup
2. Uncheck all 3 boxes for Analog line detection

This stops the printer from looking for a physical fax line. It will still work for digital fax server — it just won't alarm about the missing analog connection anymore.
Device Refresh Dashboard 2027 · Knowledge Base · Walmart Stores, Inc. — Internal Use Only
